_description: Standalone actuarial model for the Italy Post-RSP Rehabilitation Plan (italy-rsp-rehab-2026). All monetary amounts in EUR (1 unit = €1.00). Sources: policlinicogemelli.it, protesidispalla.com, chirurgiarticolare.it, prontopro.it, tiassisto.it, quotidianosanita.it (2025-2026 Italian market rates).

clinicalContext.indication: Reverse Shoulder Prosthesis (RSP / Protesi Inversa di Spalla)
primaryIndications: Rotator cuff tear arthropathy (cuff tear + glenohumeral arthritis)
Massive irreparable rotator cuff tear without arthritis (in selected cases)
Complex proximal humerus fractures (Neer 3-4 part, in patients >65)
Failed anatomical shoulder arthroplasty with rotator cuff deficiency
icd10Codes: Z96.611 (presence of right shoulder joint implant)
Z96.612 (presence of left shoulder joint implant)
S42.201 (fracture of proximal humerus, right — fracture indication)

cptCodesCovered.description: Arthroplasty, glenohumeral joint; total shoulder with or without autograft (reverse/constrained)

keyClinicaldifferencesVsTHR_TKR: STRICT 6-WEEK PROTECTIVE PHASE: no active shoulder elevation, pendulum exercises only, full-time sling. This is the most critical constraint distinguishing RSP from lower-limb prostheses.
Deltoid becomes the primary motor post-RSP, replacing the torn rotator cuff. Deltoid re-education is the cornerstone of rehab.
NO passive stretch into internal rotation for 6 weeks (subscapularis protection, when repaired).
Scapular stabilization and proprioception are critical secondary goals.
Home rehab starts earlier than lower-limb (patient ambulatory independently) but upper limb restrictions are longer.
ROM targets: forward flexion >= 120 degrees, external rotation >= 30 degrees, internal rotation to L3.
Recovery plateau typically at 6-9 months, longer than THR/TKR (3-4 months).
Fewer sessions required than TKR (no ROM stiffness risk) but higher expertise per session (deltoid motor re-education).

sources: policlinicogemelli.it — RSP procedure overview and private pricing context
protesidispalla.com (Dr. Claudio Ascani) — shoulder arthroplasty cost indicators
prontopro.it / cronoshare.it — 2026 domiciliary physio market rates
tiassisto.it — 2026 RSA/rehab facility daily rates (North-Centre Italy)

phase1_hospital.label: Surgical episode + 7-day inpatient stay
includes: Pre-operative imaging (CT scan + MRI for RSP planning)
Operating room + general anaesthesia + regional block
Reverse shoulder prosthesis implant (glenosphere + baseplate + humeral stem)
7-day inpatient stay
Wound management and haematoma prevention
Initial sling fitting and positioning instructions
Inpatient physio: pendulum exercises + scapular retraction awareness
Surgeon + specialist follow-up at discharge
phase1_hospital.rationale: Mid-to-upper range private clinic, North-Centre Italy. RSP implant is more expensive than anatomical shoulder prosthesis (glenosphere + baseplate system: approx €3,500-6,000 implant cost alone). Higher than TKR (16000) due to implant cost; lower than THR (18000) due to shorter OR time.

phase2_rehabFacility.label: Inpatient rehabilitation facility
includes: Accommodation and meals
Daily physiotherapy sessions (45-60 min)
Weeks 1-3: pendulum exercises, passive elevation to tolerance, sling management
Weeks 4-5: progressive active-assisted ROM, scapular stabilization
Week 5-end: early active deltoid strengthening (within protected range)
Nursing wound monitoring
Orthopaedic follow-up at day 21 and day 35
Pain management (TENS, cryotherapy)
phase2_rehabFacility.rationale: Same facility and daily rate as THR/TKR (€130/day x 35 days). RSP patients are ambulatory (lower limbs unaffected), which can reduce nursing intensity, but the specialized shoulder physio protocol maintains similar cost.

phase3_homeRehab.label: Home physiotherapy
includes: Domiciliary physiotherapy session (45-60 min)
Travel supplement
Progressive active ROM and deltoid motor re-education
Posterior capsule stretching (after week 6)
Functional ADL training (dressing, reaching, hair care)
Return-to-activity programme (driving from week 8, overhead from week 12)
Final outcome assessment (Constant-Murley Score)
phase3_homeRehab.rationale: 15 sessions at €80. Fewer sessions than TKR (18) because RSP home rehab is less physically demanding in weeks 5-6 (no load-bearing issues). Higher rate (€80 vs €75 for TKR) due to specialized shoulder deltoid re-education technique requiring certified shoulder physiotherapist.

comparisonWithOtherProcedures.note: RSP sits between THR and TKR. Higher implant cost than TKR but shorter surgical time than THR. Home physio fewer sessions but higher expertise rate.

rationale.utilizationRate: RSP is performed in patients typically aged 65-80 with rotator cuff tear arthropathy. Incidence of surgical indication in this cohort is ~1-2%/year. Pool targets at-risk members post-diagnosis. 5% is conservative for an enrolled symptomatic population.
rationale.bufferMargin: 20% standard buffer across all three orthopaedic plans.
rationale.poolSize: n=100 for consistency with THR/TKR plans, enabling direct premium comparison.

sponsorSeedFunding.description: One-time sponsor contribution covering ~30% of expected annual payout per tier.

lpBackstop.description: Covers ~2 additional full-100% RSP claims above expected utilization.
lpBackstop.positioning: Intermediate between TKR (400 bps) and THR (500 bps), matching RSP per-claim cost rank.

primaryOutcome.interpretation: Score 71-100 = good to excellent outcome post-RSP
primaryOutcome.note: CMS 70/100 = healthAlphaScore 7000. Lower than THR (HHS 80 = 8000) and comparable to TKR (OKS 34/48 = 7100). Shoulder scores post-RSP are harder to achieve due to the protective phase limiting early ROM gains.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: ASES (American Shoulder and Elbow Surgeons) score

protectivePhaseAlert.constraint: Strict sling immobilisation. No active elevation. Pendulum exercises only.
protectivePhaseAlert.oracleRule: Oracle must verify sling compliance at day 7, 21, and 42 via physio report hash. Active elevation reported before day 42 triggers claim review.
protectivePhaseAlert.clinicalRisk: Premature active elevation risks glenosphere-baseplate interface failure or subscapularis tear (if repaired). This is the most critical compliance checkpoint for RSP.

oracleAttestation.secondaryOracle: Optional: wearable IMU (inertial measurement unit) sensor on affected arm attesting ROM and movement patterns
oracleAttestation.quorum: 1-of-1 (single physio) or 2-of-2 (physio + wearable IMU) if device enrolled
oracleAttestation.evidencePolicy: All clinical data off-chain. Only blake3 hashes on-chain. GDPR compliant.

bondMechanic.purpose: Adherence incentive, including sling compliance. Refunded if CMS >= 70 at day 90. Redistributed if patient abandons protocol.

sensitivityAnalysis.description: Impact of varying utilization rate on premium sustainability at standard-70% tier

scenarios.note: Premium becomes unaffordable — requires sponsor subsidy or LP backstop expansion

fullCrossplanComparison.description: Complete side-by-side: THR vs TKR vs RSP on the devnet

table.RSP: Strict sling, no active elevation for 6 weeks
